title: The One With The Fake Monica

lines.text: Monica and Rachel's, everyone is looking at papers.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: How could someone get a hold of your credit card number?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I have no idea. But look how much they spent!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Monica, would you calm down? The credit card people said that you only have to pay for the stuff that you bought.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I know. It's just such reckless spending.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I think when someone steals your credit card, they've kind of already thrown caution to the wind.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Wow, what a geek. They spent $69.95 on a Wonder Mop.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That's me.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh! The yuk! Ross, he's doing it again! (Points to a lamp which is shaking behind the sofa)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Marcel, stop humping the lamp! Stop humping! Now Marcel, come back- (Marcel runs toward Rachel's room) come here, Marcel-

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh no, not in my room! I'll get him.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ross, you've got to do something about the humping.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What? It's, it's just a phase.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, that's what we said about Joey...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Would you all relax? It's not that big a deal.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (Out of shot) Stop it! Marcel! Bad monkey!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Let's just say my Curious George doll is no longer curious.
Opening Credits
Monica and Rachel's, late at night Monica is still examining her bill as Rachel emerges from her room.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, Monica. You are not still going over that thing.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: This woman's living my life.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: She's living my life, and she's doing it better than me! Look at this, look. She buys tickets for plays that I wanna see. She, she buys clothes from stores that I'm intimidated by the sales people. She spent three hundred dollars on art supplies.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You're not an artist.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, well I might be if I had the supplies! I mean, I could do all this stuff. Only I don't.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, Monica, c'mon, you do cool things.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh really? Okay, let's compare, shall we.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (Yawning) Oh, it's so late for 'Shall we'...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Do I go horseback riding in the park? Do I take classes at the New School?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (Yawning) Nooo...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: This is so unfair! She's got everything I want, and she doesn't have my mother.
Central Perk, Joey and Chandler are discussing stage names.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: How about Joey... Pepponi?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No, still too ethnic. My agent thinks I should have a name that's more neutral.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Joey... Switzerland?
The waitress brings their coffee.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Plus, y'know, I think it should be Joe. Y'know, Joey makes me sound like I'm, I dunno, this big. (Waitress looks at him funny) Which I'm not.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Joe...Joe...Joe...Stalin?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Stalin...Stalin...do I know that name? It sounds familiar.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, it does not ring a bell with me...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (Writes it down) Joe Stalin. Y'know, that's pretty good.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Might wanna try Joseph.
Joey visibly thinks 'Of course!' and writes it down.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Joseph Stalin. I think you'd remember that!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh yes! Bye Bye Birdie, starring Joseph Stalin. Joseph Stalin is the Fiddler on the Roof.
Monica and Rachel's, Monica is there as Phoebe and Rachel enter.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hi. (On the phone) Hi, uh, yes, this is Monica Geller. Um, I believe I'm taking some classes with you and I was wondering what they were.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What are you doing?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (Hushes her) Alright, great. Thanks a lot. (Hangs up) I'm going to tap class.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What, what, so that you can dance with the woman that stole your credit card?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: This woman's got my life, I should get to see who she is.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Go to the post office! I'm sure her picture's up! ...Okay, Monica, y'know what, honey, you're kinda losing it here! I mean, this is really becoming like a weird obsession thing.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: This is madness. It's madness, I tell you, for the love of God, Monica, don't do it!! ...Thank you.
A Tap Class, the girls are standing at the door.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What d'you think?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Lotsa things.
They go in and sit down.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Which one do you think she is?
The teacher comes up to them.

lines.speaker: Teacher
lines.text: May I help you?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, no thanks, we're just here to observe.

lines.speaker: Teacher
lines.text: You don't observe a dance class. You dance a dance class. Spare shoes are over there.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What does she mean?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I think she means (Imitates) 'You dance a dance class'. Oh, c'mon, c'mon. (They put on some spare shoes)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, d'y'see anybody you think could be me?

lines.speaker: Teacher
lines.text: (To the class) People! Last time there were some empty yoghurt containers lying around after class. Let's not have that happen again!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: She could be you.
Music starts

lines.speaker: Teacher
lines.text: Let's get started. Five, six, a-five six seven eight...
Everyone starts to dance in unison. Monica flounders

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, I'm not getting this!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (Dancing in a swirly, Phoebe kind of way) I'm totally getting it!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Did you ever feel like sometimes you are just so unbelievably uncoordinated?
Rachel taps into view; she is in perfect sync with the rest of the class

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What? You just click when they click.

lines.speaker: Teacher
lines.text: Alright people, now everyone grab a partner.
The girls are unsure how to pair off. Phoebe settles it

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay. And, my, dead, mother, says, you, are, it. I'm with Rachel.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Great. It's gym class all over again.

lines.speaker: Phoebe and Rachel
lines.text: Aww.

lines.speaker: Teacher
lines.text: Well that's all right, you can come up to the front and dance with me.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Why don't I just take off my clothes and have a nightmare.
She starts to walk very slowly toward the front of the room. The teacher grabs her hand and pulls her. Suddenly a woman bursts in

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: It's okay, it's okay, I'm here, I'm here. Sorry I'm late, okay, here I am. Who's the new tense girl?

lines.speaker: Teacher
lines.text: She's your partner.

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: Hi. I'm Monica.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh. Monica! ...Hi. I'm Mo- ...nana.

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: (Fake Monica) Monana?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah. It's Dutch.

lines.speaker: Fake Monica
lines.text: You're kidding! I-I spent three years in Amsterdam. (Asks her something in Dutch)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Um, Pennsylvania Dutch.

lines.speaker: Teacher
lines.text: And we're dancing. A-five, six, seven, eight...
Central Perk, Ross is entering.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (Mortified) Hi.

lines.speaker: Chandler and Joey
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Where've you been?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: At the vet.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: She's not gonna make you wear one of those big plastic cones, is she?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: She says Marcel's humping thing's not a phase. Apparently he's reached sexual maturity.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (To Chandler) Hey! He beat ya.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: She says as time goes on, he's gonna start getting agressive and violent.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So what does this mean?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm gonna have to give him up.
Commercial Break
Central Perk, scene continued from earlier.  They guys are sitting there like the Three Monkeys.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I can't believe it, Ross. This sucks!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I don't get it, I mean, you just got him. How can he be an adult already?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I know. I know. I mean, one day, he's this little thing, and before you know it, he's this little thing I can't get off my leg.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Isn't there any way you can keep him?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, no. The vet says unless he's in a place where he has regular access to some... monkey lovin,' he's just gonna get vicious. I've just gotta get him into a zoo.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: How do you get a monkey into a zoo?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I know that one! ...No, that's Popes into a Volkswagen.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, we're applying to a lot of them. Naturally our first choice would be one of the bigger state zoos, y'know, like, uh, San Diego... right? But that might just be a pipe dream, because, y'know, he's out of state. Uh, my vet, uh, knows someone at Miami, so that's a possibility.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, but that's like two blocks away from the beach. I mean, it's a total party zoo.
Phoebe, Monica, and Rachel enters.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey. We found her, we found the girl.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Did you call the cops?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Nope. We took her to lunch.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ah. Your own brand of vigilante justice.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?! Are you insane? This woman stole from you. She stole. She's a stealer.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Y'know what? After you're with this woman for like ten minutes, you forget all that. I mean, she is this astounding person, with this, with this amazing spirit.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, which she probably stole from some cheerleader.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: ...Take off their hats!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Popes in a Volkswagen! ...I love that joke.
Monica and Rachel's, Monica, Rachel and Fake Monica are there.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No way. No way did you do this.

lines.speaker: Fake Monica
lines.text: Monana was very brave.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It was so wild. We told them we were the Gunnersens in room six fifteen. Only to find out the Boston Celtics had taken over the entire sixth floor!

lines.speaker: Fake Monica
lines.text: So once they caught on to the fact that we're, y'know, short and have breasts...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: ...They threw us out! I was thrown out of a hotel! Me!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Go Monana! Well, you ladies are not the only ones living the dream. I get to go pour coffee for people I don't know. Don't wait up. (Exits)

lines.speaker: Fake Monica
lines.text: Oh, by the way, tomorrow we're auditioning for a Broadway show.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: 'Scuse me?

lines.speaker: Fake Monica
lines.text: There's an open call for Cats. I'm thinking we go down there, sing Memories and make complete fools of ourselves. Whaddya say?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Nononononono. Think who you're dealing with here. I mean, I'm not like you. I-I can't even stand in front of a tap class.

lines.speaker: Fake Monica
lines.text: Well, that's just probably 'cause of your Amish background.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Fake Monica
lines.text: Well, you're Pennsylvania Dutch, right?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Right. Till I bought a blow dryer, then I was shunned.

lines.speaker: Fake Monica
lines.text: I-I used to be just like you. And then one day I saw a movie that changed my life. Did you ever see Dead Poets' Society?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Uh-huh.

lines.speaker: Fake Monica
lines.text: I thought that movie was so incredibly... boring. I mean, that thing at the end where the kid kills himself because he can't be in the play? What was that?! It's like, kid, wait a year, leave home, do some community theatre. I walked out of there and I thought, 'Now, that's two hours of my life that I'm never getting back.' And that thought scared me more than all the other crap I was afraid to do.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wow. Then I would definitely not recommend Mrs. Doubtfire.
Monica and Rachel's, tiem lapse.  Everyone but Joey and Monica are there.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (Reading letters) Oh God. (To Marcel) We didn't get into Scranton. (To the others) That was like our safety zoo. They take like dogs and cows. See? I don't know who this is harder on, me or him.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I'd say that chair's taking the brunt.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Marcel! Marcel! Marcel, no! Good boy. See, how can nobody want him?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, somebody will.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (entering) You know there already is a Joseph Stalin?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You're kidding.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Apparently he was this Russian dictator who slaughtered all these people. You'd think you would've known that!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Y'know, you'd think I would've.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Phoebe. Whaddyou think a good stage name for me would be?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: ...Flame Boy.
Central Perk, Ross is talking to Dr. Baldhara, a zookeeper.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Where exactly is your zoo?

lines.speaker: Dr. Baldhara
lines.text: Well, it's technically not a zoo per se, it's more of an interactive wildlife experience. Let me ask you some questions about, is it, uh, Marcel?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Dr. Baldhara
lines.text: Does he, uh, fight with other animals?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No-no, he's, he's very docile.

lines.speaker: Dr. Baldhara
lines.text: Even if he were... cornered?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well I, I don't know. Why?

lines.speaker: Dr. Baldhara
lines.text: Uh, how is he at handling small objects?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: He can hold a banana, if that's whatcha mean...

lines.speaker: Dr. Baldhara
lines.text: How about a hammer, or a small blade?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Why- why- why would he need a blade?

lines.speaker: Dr. Baldhara
lines.text: Well, if he's up against a jungle cat or an animal with horns, you've got to give the little guy something. Otherwise it's just cruel.
Chandler and Joey burst in, with Marcel

lines.speaker: Chandler and Joey
lines.text: He- he- he got in, he- he got in to San Diego.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: We, we come back from our walk and the- the phone was ringing...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: ...He's in.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: He's in! Oh, did you hear that, Marcel? San Diego. San Diego!

lines.speaker: Dr. Baldhara
lines.text: You're making a big mistake here. I mean, San Diego's all well and good, but if you give him to me, I'll start him off against a blind rabbit and give you twenty percent of the gains.
Monica and Rachel's, Rachel is dusting. She comes to the table, lifts all the magazines and wipes under them, then just puts them down again. Monica bursts in, obviously drunk.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yo- hooo!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Where the hell've you been?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Monica and I just crashed an embassy party.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Are you drunk?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Noooo! (Comes closer and whispers) I'm lying. I am so drunk.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh God, oh. Great, Monica, y'know what, you could've called, I have been up here, I've been worried...
Monica is drinking from the tap

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Monica? Monica!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Water rules!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes, yes, it does. Okay, look, the restaurant called, they wanna know if you're gonna be showing up for work?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Nope. Going to the Big Apple Circus today.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay Monica, what are you doing? You're gonna lose your job! This is not you!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No, it is me! Y'know, I'm not just the person who needs to fluff the pillows and pay the bills as soon as they come in! Y'know, when I'm with her, I am so much more than that. I'm- I'm Monana!
The phone rings and Rachel answers

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hello? Yes, she is, hold on a second, please. Monana, it's for you, the credit card people.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Helloooo? Yeah. Oh my God. Thanks.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: They've arrested Monica.
New York City Department of Correction, Monica is visiting Fake Monica.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Fake Monica
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: How are you?

lines.speaker: Fake Monica
lines.text: I'm not too bad. Fortunately, blue's my colour. How-how did you know I was here?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Because... I'm Monica Geller. It was my credit card you were using.

lines.speaker: Fake Monica
lines.text: That I was not expecting.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I want you to know, it wasn't me who turned you in.

lines.speaker: Fake Monica
lines.text: Oh. Thanks.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No, thank you! You have given me so much! I mean, if it wasn't for you, I would never have gotten to sing Memories on the stage at the Wintergarden Theater!

lines.speaker: Fake Monica
lines.text: Well, actually, you only got to sing 'Memo-'.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I just can't believe you're in here. I mean, what am I gonna do without you? Who's gonna crash the embassy parties with me? Who's gonna take me to the Big Apple Circus?

lines.speaker: Fake Monica
lines.text: Monica, I started my day by peeing in front of twenty-five other women, and you're worried about who's gonna take you to the Big Apple Circus?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, not... worried, just... wondering.

lines.speaker: Fake Monica
lines.text: There's nothing to wonder about, Monica. You're gonna go back to being exactly who you were, because that's who you are.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Not necessarily...

lines.speaker: Fake Monica
lines.text: Yes necessarily! I mean, I dunno what it is, maybe it's the Amish thing.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Um, I'm not actually Amish.

lines.speaker: Fake Monica
lines.text: Really? Then why are you like that?
Tap Class, Monica is standing by the door.

lines.speaker: Teacher
lines.text: You by the door. In or out?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: In. (She joins in the dancing. She still flounders)

lines.speaker: Teacher
lines.text: You in the back, you're getting it all wrong!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, but at least I'm doing it!
The Airport, everyone but Monica is there to see off Marcel.

lines.speaker: PA
lines.text: This is the final boarding call for flight 67 to San Diego, boarding at gate 42A.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay. Good-bye, little monkey guy. Alright, I wrote you this poem. Okay, but don't eat it 'till you get on the plane.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Aww. Thank you, Aunt Phoebe.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, bye, champ. Now, I know there's gonna be a lot of babes in San Diego, but remember, there's also a lot to learn.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I dunno what to say, Ross. Uh, it's a monkey.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Just, just say what you feel.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Marcel, I'm hungry.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That was good.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (Brings Marcel a teddy bear) Marcel, this is for you. It's, uh, just, y'know, something to, um, do on the plane.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh, if you guys don't mind, I'd like to take a moment, just me and him.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Oh, sure. Sure, absolutely. (They just stand there, then realise what he means and go to the other end of the room)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Marcel, c'mere, c'mere. (He sits down and Marcel jumps down and sits beside him) Well buddy, this is it. There's just a coupla things I want to say. I'm really gonna miss you, and I'm never gonna forget about you. You've been more than just a pet to me, you've been more like a be- (Marcel climbs down and starts humping his leg) Okay, Marcel, please, could you leave my leg alone? Could you just stop humping me for two seconds?! Marcel, would- okay, just take him away. Just take him.
Marcel is put in a cage and taken away.
Closing Credits
A Theater, there is a casting session going on for a play.

lines.speaker: Actor
lines.text: (Very melodramatically, and very badly) Oh, that I were a glove upon that hand, that I might... touch thy cheek...
Casting Director No. 1: That's fine, thank you.
Casting Director No. 2: Next. (Joey walks onstage

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hi, uh, I'll be reading for the role of Mercutio.
Casting Director No. 2: Name?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Holden McGroin.
End
